entities.name: HETEROSIS
entities.description: Phenotypic and functional superiority manifested in F1 crosses over the parents

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop where heterosis has been extensively exploited for yield and productivity

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A nutritious cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) capable of growing in harsh and marginal environments

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced, which is enhanced through the exploitation of heterosis

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The efficiency of crop production enhanced by single-cross hybrids

entities.name: MARGINAL ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Harshest and most marginal environments where pearl millet can grow

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC MALE-STERILITY (CMS) SYSTEM
entities.description: A system used in pearl millet to exploit heterosis via a three-lines breeding system

entities.name: THREE-LINES BREEDING SYSTEM
entities.description: A breeding system involving A-, B-, and R-lines to develop hybrids

entities.name: TIFTON, GEORGIA, USA
entities.description: The location where the first male-sterile lines Tift 23A and Tift18A were developed

entities.name: PUNJAB AGRICULTURAL UNIVERSITY
entities.description: The institution in Ludhiana, India, that developed the first single-cross pearl millet hybrid

entities.name: HYBRID BAJRA 1
entities.description: The first single-cross pearl millet hybrid developed in 1965

entities.name: ADAPTABILITY
entities.description: The ability of pearl millet hybrids to thrive in various environments

entities.name: WHOLE GENOME SEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of determining the complete DNA sequence of an organism's genome

entities.name: TIFT 23D2B1
entities.description: A specific pearl millet genome that underwent whole genome sequencing

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, which led a genome re-sequencing consortium

entities.name: GENOMICS
entities.description: The study of the complete set of genes in an organism to understand genetic architecture

entities.name: MOLECULAR TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies used to provide insights into genetic architecture and heterotic gene pools

entities.name: HETEROTIC GENE POOLS
entities.description: Groups of genes that contribute to the manifestation of heterosis

entities.name: WHOLE-GENOME PREDICTION MODELS
entities.description: Models incorporating gene pools, mapped traits, and markers to advance heterosis breeding

entities.name: GENE MARKERS
entities.description: Genetic markers used within whole-genome prediction models

relationships.description: location of development of male-sterile lines for
